architecture.summary: Prover-Verifier Deliberation (PVD) is an inference-time protocol where two frozen language models engage in a structured challenge-response dialogue over a multiple-choice question. The prover defends a candidate answer by decomposing it into atomic sub-claims and a reasoning paragraph; the verifier examines the proof transcript and issues targeted challenges or a terminal verdict (Accept, Challenge, Reject). The dialogue loops until the verifier reaches a verdict or a fatigue limit is hit. Separate conversation histories are maintained for prover and verifier; the verifier observes only the prover's structured outputs, not its internal reasoning. After each rejection, a new attempt is initiated with adversarial context from prior failures, up to a retry limit. The protocol terminates with a structured confidence verdict: Accept + No Change (ANC) — acceptance without answer revision — signals high confidence; other outcomes trigger majority-vote fallback or abstention.

biblio.title: Trust but Verify: Prover-Verifier Deliberation for Selective LLM Prediction

claims.statement: On GPQA Diamond, PVD with Claude Sonnet 4.6 prover and Haiku 4.5 verifier achieves HC-Prec 84.2% at HC-Cov 77% with roughly three LLM calls per question.

claims.metric: HC-Prec gap (ANC precision minus non-ANC accuracy)
claims.statement: ANC questions on GPQA Diamond yield a 30pp HC-Prec gap over the non-ANC complement (e.g., Sonnet/Haiku: 84.2% ANC precision vs 52.2% non-ANC accuracy).
The strongest ANC signal on GPQA Diamond comes from GPT-5.4 prover with GPT-5.4-mini verifier, yielding HC-Prec 97.6% at HC-Cov 43% with a +34.8pp gap over the complement.

On Humanity's Last Exam, weak prover-verifier pairings can collapse or invert the Accept/Reject accuracy gap: Sonnet 4.6 prover with Haiku 4.5 verifier yields negative HC-Prec gap of −6.8pp (ANC 15.2% vs non-ANC 50.0%), illustrating a practical failure mode when the verifier operates outside its effective region.

baseline.name: Self-Consistency, Universal Self-Consistency, multi-agent debate, Reflexion

claims.statement: Compared to Self-Consistency, Universal Self-Consistency, multi-agent debate, and Reflexion, PVD occupies a distinct and favorable position on the accuracy–calibration–efficiency Pareto frontier, supplying an argument-defensibility signal for selective prediction rather than merely reproducing sample agreement or debate consensus.

PVD's ANC signal and Self-Consistency's full-consensus signal overlap only partially on GPQA Diamond (54% overlap at 96.3% joint precision); each method catches a distinct class of the other's errors, with PVD sensitive to reasoning structure and SC sensitive to output consistency.
On GPQA Diamond, Reflexion's stability-based confidence signal yields HC-Prec 82.6% at HC-Cov 93%, with a +4pp gap over the unstable complement, compared to PVD's +29–35pp ANC gap.

High HC-Prec can transfer across model families (GPT and Gemini pairings), while verifier strictness and domain competence largely determine the size of the selection gap; increasing verifier capability and strictness widens the gap by enabling multi-round adversarial pressure.
